violations.text: No allergy statement on menus. English Food Allergies What You Need to Know New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (pancake and French Toast) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

Quiche, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

Ham was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Eggs, and deviled egg filling were placed hot in the prep unit in covered plastic containers, and the filling container greater than four inches deep. Filling was transferred to a shallow metal pan, and both eggs & filling were placed in walk in cooler to rapidly chill. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024
Food observed stored on shelves less than 6 inches from the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024
Employee observed drying food containers with a cloth. Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils shall be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024
A ceiling tile above the 3 bay sink is in poor repair, with a piece missing, exposing spun insulation. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The dishwasher was not in good repair, not reaching a final rinse temperature of 160 degrees F. The 3 bay sink will be used to sanitize equipment and utensils until dishwasher is sanitizing properly. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
All food contact surfaces must be cleaned, sanitized, air dried, and properly stored. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

The door to the outside, located in the dining room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

Equipment storage rack is located next to the mop sink. Equipment and utensils must be stored in a clean dry location, where they are not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination, and at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
The prep unit was not in good repair, at 78 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
All equipment and utensils must be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
Floor needs to be finished. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the establishment. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
